ul.li: Magic: Constant 0x4A524E4C ("JRNL"). Validated on mount to verify disk format compatibility.
Version: Current schema version. Incompatible versions must block filesystem mounting to prevent data corruption.
Sequence: Monotonically increasing ID for journal rotation; used to identify the latest valid header during recovery.
Head: Byte offset within the journal log file where the next log record will be written.
Tail: Byte offset of the oldest log record required for recovery (used for Checkpointing).
Checksum: CRC64-ECMA calculated over bytes [0x000, 0x020). Must be verified before updating head/tail pointers.

Alignment: The structure is explicitly padded to 4KB (standard hardware page size) to ensure atomic sector writes on modern NVMe and Advanced Format (4Kn) drives. This prevents "torn writes" of the metadata header.
